articles.title: US Manufacturing Output Stalls for the First Time This Year
articles.summary: US manufacturing production stalled in May after four months of gains, suggesting problems with supply chains disrupted by the Iran war and soaring costs may be starting to weigh on activity.

articles.title: Stocks Join Bonds Higher as US-Iran Deal Sinks Oil: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Stocks climbed around the world, joining gains in bonds as oil slumped after the US and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, fueling hopes for an end to the conflict that has roiled markets.

articles.title: US Bonds Rally as Traders Trim Fed Rate-Hike Bets on Iran Deal
articles.summary: Treasuries advanced as investors dialed back expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes following news of a deal to halt the Iran war.

articles.title: Thousands of Public-School Staff Across US Face Layoff Warnings
articles.summary: Thousands of US public-school employees have received warnings saying they could lose their jobs as the nation’s education system grapples with mounting financial pressures.

articles.title: Airport Handler Menzies Sees High Jet Fuel Prices Through Summer
articles.summary: Airport ground-services provider Menzies Aviation Ltd. expects jet fuel costs to stay high for several more months because of fallout from the Iran war.

articles.title: Euro Area Is Seeing Ratings Increasingly Converge, Scope Says
articles.summary: The euro area is experiencing a phase of convergence across the spectrum of national credit scores despite wildly varying underlying dynamics, Scope Ratings said.

articles.title: World Bank Makes Second Foray Into Emerging-Market CLO Funding
articles.summary: The World Bank’s loan-backed bond program has surpassed the $1 billion mark after the lender raised another $509 million for a program aimed at supporting job creation in developing nations.
